claims.text: Task creation is split into createQueuedTaskRun() and createRunningTaskRun() -- two separate factories for the two initial states. Queued = caller knows the task will run but hasn't started. Running = caller is the executor and is already running it. This matches WorkTrain's pattern: a session can be enqueued or running.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: WorkTrain session tracking could adopt the same two-factory pattern: createQueuedSession() when the trigger is accepted but waiting for the semaphore, createRunningSession() when runWorkflow() actually starts.

claims.text: DetachedTaskLifecycleRuntime is an interface with 8 methods (createQueuedTaskRun, createRunningTaskRun, startTaskRunByRunId, recordTaskRunProgressByRunId, finalizeTaskRunByRunId, completeTaskRunByRunId, failTaskRunByRunId, setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId). The runtime is injectable via registerDetachedTaskRuntime() and has a DEFAULT impl. Test isolation uses setDetachedTaskLifecycleRuntime() + resetDetachedTaskLifecycleRuntimeForTests().
claims.worktrain_adaptation: The runtime-injection pattern for tests is directly valuable for WorkTrain. WorkTrain's runWorkflow() currently uses DAEMON_STATS_DIR injection for the same purpose but is less systematic. A SessionLifecycleRuntime interface would consolidate: createSession, markRunning, recordProgress, finalize, markStuck.

claims.text: The task registry (Map-based in-memory, restored from SQLite on startup) is completely independent of OpenClaw's session machinery. task-registry.ts imports only from ./task-executor.js, ./task-registry.types.js, ../infra/..., and ../logging/... -- no channel, agent, or gateway imports. It IS independently instantiable.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: The task registry is portable to WorkTrain with only type substitutions. The SQLite store (task-flow-registry.store.sqlite.ts) would replace WorkTrain's execution-stats.jsonl. The Map-based in-memory layer above the store means no performance regression.

claims.text: TaskRecord has progressSummary?: string and terminalSummary?: string -- two distinct string fields for the current progress snapshot and the final outcome summary. These are separate from the task's status (queued/running/succeeded/failed). The pattern: status is the machine-readable state, summaries are human-readable annotations.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: WorkTrain's execution-stats.jsonl records only outcome and stepCount. Adding progressSummary (current step name) and terminalSummary (lastStepNotes snippet) would make the worktrain status command useful without reading the session event store.
